title: Foundation: on-disk store + node:http transport seam
body:
## Goal
Establish the shared HTTP transport and persistent on-disk store seam that every later capability slice builds on, where the boot-time route loader tolerates an absent `src/routes/` directory (zero routes) and the request context it passes to route modules carries the DB handle only (route modules import the auth guard directly once it exists).

## Changes
- {"path":"src/index.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"src/server.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"src/router.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"src/http.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"src/db.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"src/config.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"package.json","assumption":"refactors-existing"}
- {"path":"tests/foundation.test.js","assumption":"creates"}

## Acceptance
- [ ] Running `npm start` launches an HTTP server bound to the port from `process.env.PORT`, defaulting to 3000 when unset.
- [ ] A request to an unregistered route returns HTTP 404 with a JSON error body of shape `{ error: { code, message } }`; a request using an unsupported method on a route that IS registered returns HTTP 405 (exercised in `tests/foundation.test.js` via a throwaway route registered on the router).
- [ ] With no route modules present under `src/routes/`, the server still boots and serves requests (a missing `src/routes/` directory is treated as zero registered routes, not a startup error).
- [ ] Sending a malformed or oversized JSON request body returns HTTP 400 with a structured JSON error and the server process stays alive (no crash).
- [ ] package.json declares a `start` script and lists no external web-framework dependency (only Node standard-library modules are used).
- [ ] The on-disk store file is created at the configured path on first boot and reopened (data retained, not recreated) on a subsequent boot.
- [ ] `node --test tests/foundation.test.js` exits 0.

## Verify
- node --test tests/foundation.test.js (contract)
- npm run typecheck (validate)

> 🏷️ Authored with Mandrel v0.0.0 · 2026-07-12

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Provide the transport+persistence contract (router, bounded JSON parsing, error funnel, boot migration) as one reviewable unit before any domain logic lands (b: isolates the shared seam so a transport/persistence bug is caught before auth/projects/tasks build on it).","mandrel_version":"0.0.0","authored_at":"2026-07-12"} -->

---
